description: A bare `except:` or `except Exception` with no handling that hides real bugs, including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit.
body: Failure mode: a broad except catches everything and silently continues (or only logs), so genuine failures, typos in attribute names, and even Ctrl-C are swallowed and the program limps along in a corrupt state. Detection signals: errors vanish with no traceback; the process ignores KeyboardInterrupt; ruff/flake8 flags E722 bare-except. Do not use a bare `except:` to make code 'robust'. Safer fallback: catch the narrowest exception type you can actually handle, re-raise or chain with `raise ... from e` otherwise, and never catch BaseException; add a test asserting the unexpected error propagates.
